title: GroundCheck API Response Schema
description:
Defines the exact JSON structure of all GroundCheck API responses. Two top-level shapes exist: SuccessResponse (query answered) and ErrorResponse (request could not be fulfilled). Use the 'status' field to discriminate. 

OWNERSHIP NOTE: The 'confidence' object is owned by the Confidence Engine team (Xuhui & Muhit). Its field names and types are authoritative as defined in confidence_model.md v1.1 and ConfidenceResult.to_dict() in confidence/engine.py. The backend MUST pass this object through without renaming fields. Any frontend field mapping must happen client-side.

SuccessResponse.title: SuccessResponse
SuccessResponse.description: Returned when the query was processed end-to-end: retrieval succeeded, LLM generated an answer, and confidence scoring completed (possibly in degraded mode).

status.description: Discriminator field. Always 'ok' for success responses.

request_id.description: Server-generated UUID for this request. Used for log correlation, audit trail, and support tickets.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of when the response was generated.

query.description: The original query string submitted by the user, echoed back for frontend display and audit.
query.example: What is the maximum torque specification for M10 bolts in structural applications?

answer.description: The full generated answer text from the LLM. May contain markdown formatting. Frontend should render as markdown.
answer.example: The maximum torque specification for M10 class 8.8 bolts in structural applications is 45 N·m...

confidence.description: Confidence scoring result from the Confidence Engine. This object is the direct output of ConfidenceResult.to_dict() in confidence/engine.py. Field names MUST NOT be changed by the backend or API layer.

citations.description: Ordered list of retrieved document chunks used to generate the answer. Ordered by retrieval rank (rank 1 = most relevant). Between 0 and TOP_K_CHUNKS (default 5) entries.

ErrorResponse.title: ErrorResponse
ErrorResponse.description: Returned when the request could not be fulfilled. Partial failures (e.g., confidence scoring degraded) are NOT errors — they produce a SuccessResponse with confidence.degraded=true.

status.description: Discriminator field. Always 'error' for error responses.

request_id.description: Server-generated UUID for this request, even on failure. Used for log correlation.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp when the error was generated.

ConfidenceResult.title: ConfidenceResult
ConfidenceResult.description: AUTHORITATIVE CONTRACT — defined by confidence/engine.py ConfidenceResult.to_dict(). This is the direct output of the Confidence Engine. The backend passes this object through without modification. Field names must match exactly.

score.description: Final confidence score. Integer in [0, 100]. Computed as: round(100 * (0.7 * grounding_norm + 0.3 * gen_conf_norm)). In degraded mode, computed from the available signal only.

tier.description: Categorical reliability label mapped from score. HIGH: score>=70 (green, safe to use). MEDIUM: 40<=score<70 (amber, verify key claims). LOW: score<40 (red, do not rely on).

degraded.description: True if one or both scoring signals were unavailable and the score was computed from a single signal or set to 0. The frontend MUST display a visible warning when degraded=true. A degraded HIGH score is less reliable than a normal HIGH score.

warning.description: Human-readable warning message when degraded=true or a scoring anomaly occurred. Null when degraded=false and all signals computed normally. Frontend should surface this string directly.
examples: Grounding score unavailable. Using Generation Confidence only.
Generation confidence unavailable. Using Grounding Score only.
Both signals unavailable. Score set to 0.

ConfidenceSignals.title: ConfidenceSignals
ConfidenceSignals.description: Audit trail of raw signal values feeding into the final score. All fields from confidence/engine.py signals dict. Null values indicate the signal could not be computed (scorer raised an exception). Frontend should render these in a collapsible 'Score Breakdown' panel.

grounding_score.description: Signal 1 (Grounding Score): mean NLI entailment across all extracted claims against retrieved chunks. Range [0.0, 1.0]. Formula: (1/N) * sum_i(max_j(entailment(claim_i, chunk_j))). Null if grounding scorer failed.

grounding_num_claims.description: Number of atomic claims extracted from the answer by NLTK sentence splitter. 0 if the answer was too short or a refusal. Null if grounding scorer failed.

grounding_supported.description: Number of claims with max_entailment > 0.5 (the 'supported' threshold). Informational only — the actual grounding_score uses the continuous entailment values, not this binary count. Null if grounding scorer failed.

gen_confidence_raw.description: Signal 2 (Generation Confidence): mean token probability before normalization. Formula: (1/T) * sum_t(exp(logprob_t)) over non-special tokens. Null if generation confidence scorer failed.

gen_confidence_normalized.description: Signal 2 after normalization to [0,1]. Formula: clip((raw - 0.3) / 0.6, 0.0, 1.0). This normalized value is what feeds into the fusion formula. Null if generation confidence scorer failed.

gen_confidence_level.description: Categorical label for the raw generation confidence. HIGHLY_CONFIDENT: raw_mean > 0.8. MODERATE: 0.5 < raw_mean <= 0.8. UNCERTAIN: raw_mean <= 0.5. Informational — does not directly affect fusion. Null if generation confidence scorer failed.

grounding_contribution.description: Points contributed by the grounding signal to the final score. Formula: grounding_score_norm * 70. In degraded mode (gen confidence missing), this equals the full score. Always present — never null (0.0 when signal is missing or grounding=0).

gen_conf_contribution.description: Points contributed by the generation confidence signal to the final score. Formula: gen_confidence_norm * 30. In degraded mode (grounding missing), this equals the full score. Always present — never null (0.0 when signal is missing).

Citation.title: Citation
Citation.description: A retrieved document chunk used as evidence for the answer. Corresponds to one entry in the chunks list passed to the confidence engine. The claim_support array cross-references which claims this chunk best supports.

rank.description: 1-based rank by retrieval relevance score. Rank 1 is the most relevant chunk returned by the retriever.

chunk_index.description: 0-based index matching the position in the chunks[] array passed to confidence_engine.score(). Used to cross-reference with claim_support.best_supporting_chunk_idx.

text.description: The raw text of the retrieved document chunk. Frontend should display this in the citation panel so users can verify claims against source content.

retrieval_score.description: Cosine similarity score between the query embedding and this chunk embedding, as returned by the retriever (ChromaDB). Higher = more relevant to the query.

claim_support.description: Claims from the answer for which this chunk is the best supporting evidence (best_supporting_chunk_idx matches this chunk's chunk_index). Empty if no claims were best-supported by this chunk. Populated from grounding_scorer ClaimDetail objects.

SourceDocument.title: SourceDocument
SourceDocument.description: Metadata about the source document a chunk was retrieved from. Used by the frontend for citation display and for users to locate the original document.

document_id.description: Internal identifier for the document in the knowledge base (ChromaDB document ID or KB record ID). Null if not available from the retriever.

document_name.description: Human-readable file or document name. Displayed in the citation panel as the source reference.

section.description: Section, chapter, or heading within the document if available from the KB metadata. Null if not provided.

page_number.description: Page number within the document if available from the KB metadata. Null if not provided.

revision.description: Document revision or version string if available from KB metadata. Important for Boeing context — users must verify they are reading the current revision.

last_updated.description: Date the document was last updated in the knowledge base, if available. Null if not tracked.

ClaimSupport.title: ClaimSupport
ClaimSupport.description: NLI-based claim verification result. Indicates that this citation chunk is the best-supporting evidence for the given claim. Derived from ClaimDetail objects in grounding_scorer.py.

claim_text.description: The atomic claim extracted from the answer text by the grounding scorer. Corresponds to ClaimDetail.claim.

entailment_score.description: DeBERTa NLI entailment probability for this (claim, chunk) pair. This is the max entailment across all retrieved chunks — the score that made this chunk the best match for this claim. Corresponds to ClaimDetail.max_entailment.

supported.description: True if entailment_score > 0.5 (the SUPPORTED_THRESHOLD in grounding_scorer.py). Informational binary label. Corresponds to ClaimDetail.supported.

ResponseMetadata.title: ResponseMetadata
ResponseMetadata.description: Operational metadata about how the response was generated. Useful for performance monitoring, debugging, and audit.

LatencyBreakdown.title: LatencyBreakdown
LatencyBreakdown.description: Per-stage latency in milliseconds. Allows frontend and monitoring to identify which stage is slow.

total.description: Total wall-clock time for the request from receipt to response, in milliseconds.

retrieval.description: Time for ChromaDB vector search to return chunks, in milliseconds. Null if not measured.

llm_generation.description: Time for the LLM (Mistral via vLLM/Ollama) to generate the answer and logprobs, in milliseconds. Null if not measured.

grounding_scoring.description: Time for DeBERTa NLI to compute grounding score across all (claim, chunk) pairs, in milliseconds. Null if not measured or scorer failed.

gen_confidence_scoring.description: Time for generation confidence computation (logprob processing + normalization), in milliseconds. Usually <1ms. Null if not measured.

fusion.description: Time for the weighted fusion computation, in milliseconds. Usually <1ms. Null if not measured.

ModelInfo.title: ModelInfo
ModelInfo.description: Information about the LLM and NLI models used to generate this response.

llm_model.description: Model identifier for the LLM used to generate the answer. Matches VLLM_MODEL or OLLAMA_MODEL from config.py.

llm_backend.description: Which LLM serving backend was used. 'vllm' = HPC cluster (production), 'ollama' = local dev.

nli_model.description: Model identifier for the NLI model used for grounding scoring. Matches NLI_MODEL from config.py.

confidence_model_version.description: Version of the confidence scoring model specification applied (from confidence_model.md). Enables audit traceability.

RetrieverInfo.title: RetrieverInfo
RetrieverInfo.description: Information about the retrieval configuration used for this query.

chunks_retrieved.description: Number of chunks actually returned by the retriever for this query. May be less than top_k if the KB has fewer matching documents.

top_k.description: The maximum number of chunks requested from the retriever (TOP_K_CHUNKS from config.py, default 5). Null if not tracked.

collection.description: ChromaDB collection name queried. Null if not available or not using ChromaDB.

ApiError.title: ApiError
ApiError.description: Structured error information. NOTE: Confidence scoring failures (degraded mode) do NOT produce ErrorResponse — they produce SuccessResponse with confidence.degraded=true. ErrorResponse is reserved for failures that prevent any answer from being generated.

code.description: Machine-readable error code. Frontend should handle each code with an appropriate UI state. See error_code_guide below for details.

message.description: Human-readable error message suitable for display. Should not expose internal stack traces or system paths.

details.description: Optional structured details for debugging. Not intended for display to end users. Null in production; may be populated in dev/staging.

retry_after_ms.description: For transient errors (LLM_UNAVAILABLE, LLM_TIMEOUT, RETRIEVAL_TIMEOUT): suggested wait time in milliseconds before retrying. Null if retry is not applicable or timing is unknown.

error_code_guide._note: This block is documentation only — not part of the JSON Schema validation. It describes each error code and the recommended frontend behavior.

INVALID_REQUEST.cause: Query is empty, malformed, or fails server-side validation.
INVALID_REQUEST.frontend_action: Show inline validation error. Do not retry.

QUERY_TOO_LONG.frontend_action: Show character limit warning. Do not retry.

LLM_UNAVAILABLE.cause: vLLM (HPC) or Ollama (dev) server is not reachable or returned a non-200 response.
LLM_UNAVAILABLE.frontend_action: Show 'AI model temporarily unavailable' message. Offer retry after retry_after_ms.

LLM_TIMEOUT.cause: LLM generation exceeded VLLM_TIMEOUT (120s) or OLLAMA_TIMEOUT (120s) from config.py.
LLM_TIMEOUT.frontend_action: Show 'Request timed out' message. Offer retry.

RETRIEVAL_FAILURE.cause: ChromaDB query failed (connection error, corrupted index, or invalid collection).
RETRIEVAL_FAILURE.frontend_action: Show 'Document search unavailable' message. Log for ops team.

RETRIEVAL_TIMEOUT.frontend_action: Show 'Document search timed out' message. Offer retry.

INTERNAL_ERROR.cause: Unexpected server-side exception not covered by the above codes.
INTERNAL_ERROR.frontend_action: Show generic error with request_id so the user can report it.

high_confidence_success.query: What is the maximum torque specification for M10 bolts in structural applications?
high_confidence_success.answer: The maximum torque specification for M10 class 8.8 bolts in structural applications is 45 N·m. This applies to dry-thread conditions. For lubricated threads, apply a torque reduction factor of 0.75.

citations.text: Table 4: Fastener Specifications (class 8.8) — M10 bolt maximum torque: 45 N·m (dry). For lubricated thread conditions apply torque reduction factor K=0.75.

claim_support.claim_text: The maximum torque specification for M10 class 8.8 bolts in structural applications is 45 N·m.

citations.text: Thread lubrication affects effective clamping force. When lubricants are used, torque values from Table 4 must be adjusted using the K-factor specified in Section 6.2.

claim_support.claim_text: For lubricated threads, apply a torque reduction factor of 0.75.

low_confidence_success.query: What are the overtime approval requirements for exempt employees?
low_confidence_success.answer: Exempt employees are not entitled to overtime pay under the FLSA. Supervisors may approve additional compensation via Form HR-441 for hours exceeding 50 per week.

citations.text: Systems Engineering Process: requirements analysis, functional decomposition, design synthesis, verification planning.

degraded_mode_success.query: What are the torque specs for M12 bolts?
degraded_mode_success.answer: M12 class 8.8 bolts have a maximum torque of 78 N·m in dry conditions.

confidence.warning: Grounding score unavailable. Using Generation Confidence only.

error.message: The AI model is temporarily unavailable. Please try again in a moment.

cross_team_contract._note: This block is documentation only. It records the field-level contract between the Confidence Engine team and the API/Frontend teams.
shared_ownership: citations[].claim_support — populated by backend using ClaimDetail objects from grounding_scorer; structure defined by Confidence Engine team
cross_team_contract.immutable_fields: The confidence.signals field names (grounding_score, grounding_num_claims, grounding_supported, gen_confidence_raw, gen_confidence_normalized, gen_confidence_level, grounding_contribution, gen_conf_contribution) MUST NOT be renamed. They are the direct output of ConfidenceResult.to_dict() in confidence/engine.py. Any change requires a confidence_model.md version bump and re-approval by both teams.
